example.semantic: Frontend records the branch guard plan.
example.emitted: Emitter consumes the branch plan instead of rediscovering the typeof pattern.
example.invariant: Guard dispatch cannot fall back to stmt.thenNarrowings or source-pattern detectors.

example.semantic: Unknown member access cannot opt out through permissive IR flags.
example.emitted: No C# is emitted unless the access is statically proven.
example.invariant: Permissive escape names are banned from product IR.

example.semantic: Binding metadata owns the CLR identity.
example.emitted: The emitter receives resolved metadata instead of inferring from the spelling Buffer.
example.invariant: Semantic binding payloads are validated before use.

example.source: const selected: A | B = condition ? a : b;
example.semantic: Expected-type adaptation owns the cross-file decision.
example.emitted: Call sites route through the shared planner.
example.invariant: Entrypoints cannot import raw runtime-union adapters directly.

example.semantic: Frontend guard shape chooses the branch behavior.
example.emitted: Emitter dispatches from the recorded guard shape.
example.invariant: Post-condition fallback detectors are banned.

example.semantic: Frontend CLR identity key is canonical.
example.emitted: Backend AST comparison delegates CLR identity parsing to the frontend helper.
example.invariant: Backend AST utilities cannot maintain a second CLR generic-arity parser.

example.source: A new branch-plan selector replaces an old source-pattern selector.
example.semantic: Only the new selector remains in product code.
example.emitted: No bridge or compatibility path runs.
example.invariant: Tracker-level banned product patterns are scanned in CI tests.

example.source: A compiler invariant is declared done.
example.semantic: The tracker points to a named invariant test.
example.emitted: The full test run exercises the invariant directly.
example.invariant: Catch-all regression files are forbidden as proof tests.

example.semantic: Semantic union identity and storage carrier identity are separate.
example.emitted: Storage-compatible adaptation routes through the shared planner.
example.invariant: Direct storage imports are confined to planner boundaries.

example.semantic: Lowering is chosen after semantic shape is known.
example.emitted: Runtime carrier adaptation is not invoked from arbitrary expression entrypoints.
example.invariant: Raw lowering imports are confined.

example.semantic: Union arm selection is computed once in frontend IR.
example.emitted: Emitter consumes the arm plan.
example.invariant: Branch plan tests cover discriminator narrowing.

example.source: A test constructs an if statement.
example.semantic: The test must construct emittable IR.
example.emitted: The emitter receives strict IR, not normalized legacy test IR.
example.invariant: The normalization shim is deleted.

example.semantic: Adaptation reports explicit success, reject, unchanged, or unsupported.
example.emitted: The emitter does not silently continue after unknown failure.
example.invariant: Adapt-result model is a first-class module.

example.source: A unit test and CLI compile the same branch.
example.semantic: Both use the same emittable branch-plan shape.
example.emitted: No test-only normalization changes emitter input.
example.invariant: Test IR is an alias of IrModule.

example.source: A helper name suggests broad pass-through.
example.semantic: The helper must be renamed or removed before product use.
example.emitted: No permissive helper enters codegen.
example.invariant: Forbidden helper names are scanned.

example.semantic: The active surface decides whether JS APIs are present.
example.emitted: On CLR-only surface, diagnostics are emitted from surface metadata.
example.invariant: features.ts cannot own JavaScript name lists.

example.source: A report item is marked complete.
example.semantic: The completion tracker names proof tests and examples.
example.emitted: The test runner validates the tracker before merge.
example.invariant: Every complete item has existing proof files.

example.semantic: Package metadata preserves source semantic shape.
example.emitted: Emitter does not reconstruct from compressed names.
example.invariant: Binding payload invariants validate semantic metadata.

example.semantic: Backend capability metadata declares support status.
example.emitted: Unsupported capabilities become diagnostics, not ICEs.
example.invariant: Feature-gating tests assert capability diagnostics.

example.semantic: Overload family metadata keeps one family shape.
example.emitted: Call resolution consumes canonical overload metadata.
example.invariant: Binding payload tests validate overload families.

example.semantic: Symbol semantic type and runtime storage type are distinct.
example.emitted: Storage adaptation is centralized.
example.invariant: Direct storage lookups are confined to allowed boundaries.

example.semantic: Frontend selects the narrowed arm.
example.emitted: Runtime-union modules materialize carriers, not semantic decisions.
example.invariant: Raw runtime-union imports are confined.

example.semantic: Workspace graph records source, package, and surface inputs.
example.emitted: The graph root fingerprint changes when an input changes.
example.invariant: Dependency graph tests assert deterministic fingerprints and edges.

example.source: type Point = { x: number; y: number };
example.semantic: Named structural types retain declared origin.
example.emitted: Inline anonymous shapes and named shapes lower through distinct policy.
example.invariant: Anonymous lowering tests preserve structural origin.

example.source: The compiler pipeline processes source through named phases.
example.semantic: Architecture score is derived from completed invariant items.
example.emitted: No compatibility bridge is accepted as proof.
example.invariant: The completion tracker requires all items complete with proof.
